Partially true. Historical records show the Founders did emphasize armed militias as a check against tyranny—this was a key purpose. However, the Amendment's intent was more complex. The Supreme Court in Heller (2008) ruled it also protects individual self-defense rights, not just resistance to tyranny. The phrase "bear arms" in that era primarily referred to military service. So Kirk captures one important historical rationale but oversimplifies a multifaceted provision.
